title: Genesis: AgentScribe Implementation
description:
## Genesis Bead
Tied to plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md

## Overview
AgentScribe is a Rust CLI binary that scrapes conversation logs from multiple coding agent types (Claude Code, Aider, OpenCode, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf), normalizes them into a canonical format, stores them as flat files with a Tantivy search index, and provides a query interface for agents seeking past solutions.

## Progress
- [x] Phase 1: Plugin System, Scraping & Normalization (agentscribe-mkg) — COMPLETE
- [ ] Phase 2: Tantivy Indexing & Search (agentscribe-2dp.1) — IN PROGRESS
  - [x] Tantivy schema + document builder (agentscribe-spl, agentscribe-dvs)
  - [x] Tag extraction pipeline (agentscribe-ith, agentscribe-irx, agentscribe-dq1)
  - [x] Index management CLI (agentscribe-lbg)
  - [x] Search command with BM25 + filters (agentscribe-qn8)
  - [x] Status command (agentscribe-ywm)
  - [ ] Fuzzy search (agentscribe-2dp.1.1)
  - [ ] MoreLikeThis search (agentscribe-2dp.1.2)
  - [ ] Token-budget knapsack (agentscribe-2dp.1.3)
  - [ ] Content truncation policy (agentscribe-2dp.1.4)
  - [ ] Incremental indexing (agentscribe-2dp.1.5)
  - [ ] Phase 2 integration tests (agentscribe-2dp.1.6)
- [x] Phase 3: Enrichment & Intelligence (agentscribe-usx) — COMPLETE
- [ ] Phase 4: Daemon Mode & MCP (agentscribe-slv)
  - [ ] Daemon core (agentscribe-slv.1)
  - [ ] File watcher + debounce (agentscribe-slv.2)
  - [ ] MCP server (agentscribe-slv.3)
  - [ ] Systemd service (agentscribe-slv.4)
  - [ ] Concurrent access / file locking (agentscribe-slv.5)
- [ ] Phase 5: SQLite Format Support & Extended Agents (agentscribe-zef)
  - [ ] SQLite format parser (agentscribe-zef.1)
  - [ ] Cursor + Windsurf plugins (agentscribe-zef.2)
  - [ ] Community plugins + git auto-commit (agentscribe-zef.3)
- [ ] Phase 6: Analytics, Knowledge Synthesis & Shell Integration (agentscribe-70w)
  - [ ] Recurring problem detection (agentscribe-70w.1)
  - [ ] Agent effectiveness analytics (agentscribe-70w.2)
  - [ ] Auto-generated project rules (agentscribe-70w.3)
  - [ ] Weekly digest (agentscribe-70w.4)
  - [ ] Search-on-error shell hook (agentscribe-70w.5)
  - [ ] Session lifecycle / gc (agentscribe-70w.6)

## Cross-Cutting
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline (agentscribe-egl)
- [ ] End-to-end integration test suite (agentscribe-rrn)
- [ ] Release packaging & distribution (agentscribe-3xl)
- [ ] Documentation & README (agentscribe-9un)

## Status
Phases 1 and 3 complete. Phase 2 core done, remaining: fuzzy search, MoreLikeThis, token-budget, truncation, incremental indexing, integration tests. Phases 4-6 and cross-cutting work pending.

title: Phase 2: Tantivy Indexing & Search
description:
## Phase 2: Tantivy Indexing & Search

### Objectives
- Build Tantivy index from normalized sessions (BM25 scoring, faceted fields)
- Implement incremental indexing on scrape (no full rebuild required)
- Build three-tier tag extraction pipeline (explicit, structural, keyword list)
- Implement fuzzy search via Tantivy's Levenshtein term queries
- Implement "more like this" search via Tantivy's MoreLikeThisQuery
- Build context budget packing with greedy knapsack algorithm
- Implement structured JSON output mode for agent consumption

### CLI Commands
- `agentscribe index rebuild|stats|optimize` — manage the Tantivy search index
- `agentscribe search` — query interface (primary interface for agents)
- `agentscribe status` — show tracked agents, session counts, daemon state

### Tantivy Index Schema
See plan.md lines 347-391 for the full schema definition. Key fields:
- Full-text: `content`, `summary`, `solution_summary`, `code_content`
- Faceted: `session_id`, `source_agent`, `project`, `tags`, `outcome`, `error_fingerprint`, `file_paths`, `git_commits`, `doc_type`
- Code artifacts: `code_language`, `code_file_path`, `code_is_final`
- Date/numeric: `timestamp`, `turn_count`

### Tag Extraction Pipeline (Three-Tier)
1. **Explicit:** tool names from `tool_call` events (`Edit`, `Bash`, `Read`), languages from code fence markers (`` ```rust `` → `rust`)
2. **Structural:** file extensions from `file_paths` (`.rs` → `rust`, `.py` → `python`), command names from Bash content (`docker`, `git`, `npm`, `cargo`, `kubectl`), error types from `error_fingerprints`
3. **Keyword list:** match content against bundled technology dictionary (~200 terms: frameworks, databases, cloud services, protocols). Exact word-boundary matches only.

### Search Features
- Full-text BM25 search across session content and summaries (default mode)
- Error lookup: `--error <pattern>` against normalized error fingerprints
- Code search: `--code <query> [--lang <lang>]` for code artifacts
- Solution-only: `--solution-only` to return only extracted solutions
- Similar sessions: `--like <session-id>` using Tantivy MoreLikeThis
- Filters: `--agent`, `--project`, `--since`/`--before`, `--tag`, `--outcome`, `--type`, `--model`
- Output: `--max-results`, `--snippet-length`, `--token-budget <n>` (greedy knapsack)
- Structured: `--json` for agent consumption

### Content Truncation Policy
- `user` and `assistant` content: indexed in full
- `tool_call` content: indexed in full
- `tool_result` content: truncated to first 1000 chars per event
- Total document content capped at 500KB (trim middle if exceeded: keep first 250KB + last 250KB)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tantivy index creation with full schema (all fields from plan.md)
- [ ] One document per session, concatenated with role prefixes
- [ ] Content truncation policy implemented (1000 chars for tool_result, 500KB total cap)
- [ ] Incremental indexing via `IndexWriter::add_document()` on scrape
- [ ] `agentscribe index rebuild` recreates index from session files
- [ ] `agentscribe index stats` shows document count, index size, segment info
- [ ] `agentscribe index optimize` compacts segments
- [ ] Three-tier tag extraction pipeline (explicit, structural, keyword list)
- [ ] Bundled technology keyword dictionary (~200 terms)
- [ ] Full-text BM25 search with default session-only results
- [ ] Fuzzy search via Levenshtein term queries
- [ ] `--like <session-id>` MoreLikeThis search
- [ ] `--error <pattern>` error fingerprint search
- [ ] `--code <query> [--lang <lang>]` code artifact search
- [ ] `--solution-only` filter
- [ ] All filter flags: `--agent`, `--project`, `--since`/`--before`, `--tag`, `--outcome`, `--type`, `--model`
- [ ] `--json` structured output mode
- [ ] `--token-budget <n>` greedy knapsack context packing
- [ ] `--max-results` and `--snippet-length` basic sizing
- [ ] `agentscribe status` command
- [ ] doc_type facet filtering (sessions vs code_artifacts, never mixed)

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md (lines 347-391 for index schema, lines 412-423 for phase scope, lines 465-510 for search interface)
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md
- Phase 1 bead: agentscribe-mkg
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: Tantivy indexing, BM25 search, fuzzy/MLT/error/code search, three-tier tag extraction, greedy knapsack, JSON output, index rebuild/stats/optimize, agentscribe status. All 335 tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Fuzzy search via Levenshtein term queries
description:
Implement fuzzy/typo-tolerant search using Tantivy's Levenshtein automata.

## Scope
- Add `--fuzzy` flag (or automatic fuzzy fallback when exact match returns 0 results)
- Use Tantivy's `FuzzyTermQuery` with configurable edit distance (default 1)
- Apply to full-text fields: content, summary, solution_summary

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `agentscribe search --fuzzy "postgers"` matches sessions containing "postgres"
- [ ] Edit distance configurable via flag or config.toml
- [ ] Performance stays under 100ms for typical queries
close_reason: Fuzzy search via Levenshtein term queries was already fully implemented. The --fuzzy flag enables FuzzyTermQuery across content, summary, and solution_summary fields. Edit distance is configurable via --edit-distance flag (default 1) or config.toml fuzzy_edit_distance. Automatic fuzzy fallback triggers when exact search returns 0 results. Integration test passes.

title: MoreLikeThis similar session search
description:
Implement --like <session-id> flag using Tantivy's built-in MoreLikeThisQuery.

## Scope
- Extract most significant terms from source document by TF-IDF weight
- Query index for documents with similar term distributions
- `agentscribe search --like <session-id> [--json] [-n <max>]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --like flag accepted and validated (session must exist)
- [ ] Returns sessions ranked by term similarity
- [ ] Works across agents and projects (cross-agent discovery)
- [ ] Combines with other filters (--agent, --project, --since)
close_reason: Implemented --like <session-id> MoreLikeThis similar session search. TF-IDF term extraction from content/summary/tags, top-20 terms as boosted BooleanQuery, cross-agent/project support, combines with --agent/--project/--since filters. Also added automatic fuzzy fallback for zero-result text queries. 4 unit tests passing.

title: Context budget packing (token-budget knapsack)
description:
Implement --token-budget <n> greedy knapsack algorithm for optimal context packing.

## Scope
- Token estimation: ceil(chars / 4)
- Greedy knapsack: rank by relevance, pack until budget full
- Adaptive: choose between fewer results with longer snippets vs more results with shorter snippets
- Replaces --max-results + --snippet-length when specified

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --token-budget flag accepted
- [ ] Results fit within specified token budget
- [ ] Maximizes total relevance coverage within budget
- [ ] Works with --solution-only (shorter results = more fit)
- [ ] JSON output includes token counts per result
close_reason: done

title: Content truncation policy
description:
Implement content truncation per plan spec for Tantivy document builder.

## Scope
- user/assistant content: indexed in full
- tool_call content: indexed in full
- tool_result content: truncated to first 1000 chars per event
- Total document content capped at 500KB (trim middle: keep first 250KB + last 250KB)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tool_result events truncated to 1000 chars in index documents
- [ ] Documents exceeding 500KB are middle-trimmed
- [ ] Full content preserved in flat JSONL files (truncation is index-only)
- [ ] Truncation logged at debug level
close_reason: done

title: Incremental indexing on scrape
description:
Wire Tantivy IndexWriter::add_document() into the scrape pipeline for incremental updates.

## Scope
- On scrape: index new/updated sessions immediately (no full rebuild)
- Session update: delete old doc by session_id, add updated doc
- Concurrent access: CLI searches work while daemon scrapes
- IndexWriter held only during active scrape/commit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `agentscribe scrape` indexes new sessions automatically
- [ ] Re-scraping updated sessions deletes old + adds new document
- [ ] Concurrent search during scrape returns consistent results
- [ ] No full index rebuild required for normal operation
close_reason: done

title: Phase 2 integration tests
description:
Comprehensive test suite for the indexing and search pipeline.

## Scope
- Unit tests for tag extraction (all three tiers)
- Unit tests for document builder and content truncation
- Integration tests: scrape → index → search roundtrip
- Search accuracy tests with known session data
- Filter combination tests (--agent, --project, --since, --tag, --outcome)
- JSON output format validation
- Edge cases: empty sessions, very large sessions, Unicode content

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >80% code coverage on src/index.rs, src/search.rs, src/tags.rs
- [ ] Integration test with sample Claude Code + Aider sessions
- [ ] All search modes tested (BM25, error, code, solution-only, more-like-this)
- [ ] All filter flags tested individually and in combination
- [ ] `cargo test` passes with no failures
close_reason: Phase 2 integration tests complete. Added 23 new tests covering all search modes, all filter flags individually and combined, JSON output validation, and edge cases. 161 tests pass with 0 failures. Commit: b01991f

title: Release packaging and distribution
description:
Binary distribution and installation.

## Scope
- cargo install agentscribe (crates.io publish)
- Pre-built binaries for Linux x86_64, aarch64; macOS x86_64, aarch64
- Install script: curl -sSf https://... | sh
- Shell completions: agentscribe completions bash|zsh|fish
- Man page generation (optional)
- Versioning: semver, CHANGELOG.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo install agentscribe` works from crates.io
- [ ] Pre-built binaries for all four targets
- [ ] Shell completions generate valid output for all three shells
- [ ] Install script detects platform and downloads correct binary
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md maintained with each release
close_reason: done

title: Phase 6: Analytics, Knowledge Synthesis & Shell Integration
description:
Implement analytics, knowledge synthesis, and shell integration features.

## Deliverables

### Recurring Problem Detection
- Group error fingerprints by frequency (GROUP BY fingerprint HAVING count >= 3)
- `agentscribe recurring [--since <date>] [--threshold <n>]` command
- Output: frequency, affected projects, which agents solved it, last fix
- Files: src/analytics/recurring.rs, src/cli/recurring.rs

### Agent Effectiveness Analytics
- Success rate per agent, average turns/tokens per resolution
- Problem type classification (debug/feature/refactor/investigation/configuration/documentation)
- Cost efficiency metrics using [cost.models] from config.toml
- Trend analysis over time
- `agentscribe analytics [--agent <name>] [--project <path>] [--since <date>]`
- Files: src/analytics/effectiveness.rs, src/cli/analytics.rs

### Auto-Generated Project Rules
- Extract user corrections, tool preferences, architecture conventions
- Output formats: --format claude (CLAUDE.md), --format cursor (.cursorrules), --format aider (.aider.conf.yml)
- `agentscribe rules <project-path> [--format claude|cursor|aider]`
- Files: src/analytics/rules.rs, src/cli/rules.rs

### File Knowledge Map Enhancements
- Add "known gotchas" section from anti-patterns filtered to the file
- Files: src/analytics/file_knowledge.rs

### Weekly Digest
- `agentscribe digest [--since 7d] [--output <path>]`
- Sessions completed, problems solved, recurring issues, agent comparison
- Most-touched files, token usage trends
- Markdown output suitable for 2-minute skim
- Files: src/analytics/digest.rs, src/cli/digest.rs

### Search-on-Error Shell Hook
- `agentscribe shell-hook bash|zsh|fish` generates shell integration
- PROMPT_COMMAND/precmd hook detects non-zero exit, queries fingerprint index
- Background subprocess, never blocks shell
- Files: src/cli/shell_hook.rs, templates/shell_hook_{bash,zsh,fish}.sh

### Session Lifecycle Management (GC)
- `agentscribe gc [--older-than <duration>] [--dry-run]`
- Delete normalized session files, remove Tantivy documents, compact index
- Respect max_session_age_days from config.toml
- Files: src/cli/gc.rs

## Testing
- Test recurring detection with synthetic fingerprint data
- Test analytics accuracy with known session outcomes
- Test shell hook generation for all three shells
- Test gc --dry-run shows deletions without acting
- Test rules generation produces valid CLAUDE.md/cursorrules/aider.conf.yml

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe recurring` surfaces problems solved 3+ times in the window
- `agentscribe analytics` shows per-agent success rates and cost efficiency
- `agentscribe rules` generates valid agent-specific rules files
- `agentscribe digest --since 7d` produces readable Markdown summary
- Shell hook triggers background search on command failure without blocking
- `agentscribe gc --older-than 90d` correctly removes old sessions and recompacts index
close_reason: Phase 6 complete: analytics, knowledge synthesis, shell integration. 430 tests passing, zero clippy warnings.

title: Recurring problem detection
description:
Surface problems solved 3+ times within a configurable window.

## Scope
- Error fingerprint frequency analysis (GROUP BY fingerprint, HAVING count >= threshold)
- Default window: 30 days, default threshold: 3
- Output: frequency, affected projects, which agents solved it, last fix applied
- `agentscribe recurring [--since <date>] [--threshold <n>]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly groups fingerprints by frequency
- [ ] Configurable time window and threshold
- [ ] Shows affected projects and agent breakdown
- [ ] Links to most recent fix session
- [ ] --json output mode for automation
close_reason: done

title: Agent effectiveness analytics
description:
Cross-agent performance comparison and analytics.

## Metrics
- Success rate per agent
- Average turns and tokens per successful outcome
- Specialization by problem type (debug/feature/refactor/investigation/configuration/documentation)
- Trends over time (model updates, prompt changes)
- Cost efficiency (outcome quality per dollar, using [cost.models] from config.toml)

## Problem Type Classification (rule-based)
- debug: error fingerprints present, or matches fix|bug|error|crash
- feature: Write tool creates new files, or matches add|implement|create|build
- refactor: matches refactor|rename|move|extract|clean up, no new files
- investigation: Read:Edit ratio >3:1, or matches explain|how does|what is
- configuration: files_touched includes .toml/.yaml/.json/.env/Dockerfile/Makefile
- documentation: files_touched includes .md/.rst, or matches document|readme|changelog

## CLI
- `agentscribe analytics [--agent <name>] [--project <path>] [--since <date>]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All five metrics computed correctly
- [ ] Problem type classification assigns primary + secondary types
- [ ] Cost calculations use config.toml model pricing (null model excluded)
- [ ] --json output for programmatic consumption
- [ ] Human-readable table output for terminal
close_reason: Implemented full agent effectiveness analytics: all 5 metrics, primary+secondary problem type classification, config.toml cost pricing (null model excluded), --json output, and human-readable table output. All 18 analytics tests pass.

title: Auto-generated project rules
description:
Distill session patterns into agent-specific rules files.

## Extraction Sources
- Explicit user corrections ("don't use X, use Y") → rule
- Repeated tool preferences (pnpm over npm) → convention
- Architecture patterns (test directory, ORM, framework) → context
- Known pitfalls from anti-pattern library → warnings

## Output Formats
- --format claude → CLAUDE.md (Markdown with conventions/known issues sections)
- --format cursor → .cursorrules (plain text, one instruction per line)
- --format aider → .aider.conf.yml (YAML with read: and message: keys)

## CLI
- `agentscribe rules <project-path> [--format claude|cursor|aider]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extracts user corrections from session content
- [ ] Identifies repeated tool/command preferences
- [ ] All three output formats generate valid files
- [ ] No LLM required (frequency-based heuristics only)
- [ ] Output is additive (doesn't overwrite existing rules files)
close_reason: Implemented agentscribe rules <project-path> [--format claude|cursor|aider] in src/rules.rs: extracts user corrections via regex, tool/command preferences via COMPETING_TOOLS frequency analysis, language context via code fence counting, test dir patterns, and recurring error fingerprints. Outputs CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules/.aider.conf.yml additively. No LLM required. 15 unit tests pass.

title: Weekly digest command
description:
Automated activity summary over a configurable period.

## Content
- Sessions completed (count by agent, project)
- Problems solved and recurring issues detected
- Agent comparison (success rates, specialization)
- Most-touched files
- New error patterns discovered
- Token usage trends and estimated costs

## CLI
- `agentscribe digest [--since 7d] [--output <path>]`
- Markdown format, skimmable in 2 minutes
- Can be triggered by cron, piped to email, or posted to Slack

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generates accurate session counts and breakdowns
- [ ] Includes recurring problems section
- [ ] Agent comparison table with success rates
- [ ] Most-touched files ranked by session count
- [ ] Token/cost summary using [cost.models] config
- [ ] --output writes to file instead of stdout
- [ ] --json structured output mode
close_reason: done

title: Search-on-error shell hook
description:
Shell integration that auto-queries AgentScribe when commands fail.

## Scope
- `agentscribe shell-hook bash|zsh|fish` generates shell snippet
- Uses PROMPT_COMMAND (bash) / precmd (zsh) to detect non-zero exit
- Passes failed command text (fc -ln -1) + exit code to search
- Background subprocess (&), never blocks the shell
- Result in temp file, printed on next prompt if available
- No stderr capture (fragile, breaks pipes)

## Setup
- `eval "$(agentscribe shell-hook bash)"` in .bashrc
- `eval "$(agentscribe shell-hook zsh)"` in .zshrc

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generates correct shell snippets for bash, zsh, fish
- [ ] Background search never blocks shell prompt
- [ ] One-line hint displayed when previous error has a known solution
- [ ] Discards result silently if search takes longer than one prompt cycle
- [ ] Config: [shell_hook] stderr_capture and background settings
close_reason: Implemented shell hook integration in src/shell_hook.rs. Generates snippets for bash (PROMPT_COMMAND + fc -ln -1), zsh (preexec/precmd hooks), and fish (fish_postexec). Background mode uses mktemp + disowned subprocess; result printed on next prompt. Blocking mode available via config. Self-recursion guard, 7 unit tests all passing.

title: Session lifecycle management (gc)
description:
Garbage collection for old sessions.

## Scope
- `agentscribe gc [--older-than <duration>] [--dry-run]`
- Deletes normalized session files older than specified age
- Removes their Tantivy documents
- Runs index optimize to compact segments
- Respects max_session_age_days from config.toml as default
- --dry-run shows what would be deleted without acting

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly identifies sessions older than threshold
- [ ] Deletes session files, summary files, anti-pattern sidecar files
- [ ] Removes corresponding Tantivy documents
- [ ] Runs index optimize after deletion
- [ ] --dry-run shows accurate preview
- [ ] Respects config.toml max_session_age_days
- [ ] Reports bytes reclaimed
close_reason: Implemented agentscribe gc command with full session lifecycle management: parse_duration() for human-readable durations, run_gc() deletes session files (.jsonl/.anti-patterns.jsonl/.summary.jsonl), removes Tantivy index entries, runs optimize() to compact segments, respects config.scrape.max_session_age_days, --dry-run preview, and reports bytes reclaimed. All 7 unit tests pass.

title: Documentation and README
description:
User-facing documentation and project README.

## Scope
- README.md: overview, installation, quick start, feature summary
- Architecture diagram (text-based or Mermaid)
- Plugin authoring guide updates (already exists, keep current)
- CLI reference updates (already exists, keep current with new commands)
- Example workflows for common use cases
- Configuration reference (all config.toml options documented)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README with installation, quick start, and feature overview
- [ ] All CLI commands documented in cli-reference.md
- [ ] All config.toml options documented
- [ ] Plugin authoring guide covers SQLite format
- [ ] Example workflows section with 5+ real-world scenarios
close_reason: done

title: Tag extraction: keyword dictionary and integration
description:
## Tag Extraction: Keyword Dictionary and Integration

Implement tier 3 (keyword dictionary matching) and integrate all three tiers into the document builder.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Modify `src/tags.rs` — add keyword dictionary and top-level extract function
- Create `data/tech_keywords.txt` — bundled keyword dictionary (~200 terms, one per line)
- Modify `src/index.rs` — call extract_tags during document building and populate tags facet

### What to Implement
- `data/tech_keywords.txt`: ~200 technology terms (frameworks: react, vue, django, flask, express, rails; databases: postgres, redis, mongodb, mysql, sqlite; cloud: aws, gcp, azure, s3, lambda, cloudflare; protocols: grpc, graphql, rest, websocket; tools: terraform, ansible, kubernetes, helm, docker; languages: not needed, covered by tiers 1-2)
- `pub fn extract_keyword_tags(content: &str, dictionary: &[String]) -> Vec<String>`
  - Word-boundary regex match only (no substring matches — "react" should not match "reactive")
  - Case-insensitive matching
  - Load dictionary once at startup (lazy_static or OnceCell)
- `pub fn extract_tags(events: &[CanonicalEvent]) -> Vec<String>` — combines all three tiers, deduplicates, sorts
- Integration: call `extract_tags` in `build_document()` and populate the `tags` facet field

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib tags::tests && test -f data/tech_keywords.txt && wc -l data/tech_keywords.txt | awk '{if ($1 >= 150) exit 0; else exit 1}'
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: "using react and postgres" matches tags ["react", "postgres"] but NOT "reactive"
- Unit test: word boundary — "rediscover" does NOT produce tag "redis"
- Unit test: case insensitive — "Docker" matches "docker"
- Unit test: combined extract_tags merges all three tiers without duplicates
- data/tech_keywords.txt exists with at least 150 terms
- Integration: build_document produces a Document with populated tags facet

title: Document builder: session-to-document conversion
description:
## Document Builder: Session-to-Document Conversion

Implement the document builder that converts normalized sessions into Tantivy documents. Second half of agentscribe-y71.

### Files to Modify
- `src/index.rs` — add document builder functions

### What to Implement
- `pub fn build_document(schema: &Schema, session: &NormalizedSession) -> tantivy::Document`
  - Concatenates conversation turns with role prefixes ("user: ...", "assistant: ...") into `content` field
  - Populates all faceted fields from session metadata
  - Sets `timestamp`, `turn_count`
  - Sets `doc_type` facet to "sessions"
- `pub fn build_code_artifact_doc(schema: &Schema, artifact: &CodeArtifact) -> tantivy::Document`
  - Sets `code_content`, `code_language`, `code_file_path`, `code_is_final`
  - Sets `doc_type` facet to "code_artifacts"
- Content truncation policy:
  - `tool_result` content capped at 1000 characters
  - Total `content` field capped at 500KB; if over, trim middle with "[...truncated...]" marker

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib index::tests::build_document
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: build_document with a 3-turn session produces a Document with correct content field containing role prefixes
- Unit test: tool_result over 1000 chars is truncated to 1000
- Unit test: content over 500KB has middle trimmed with marker
- Unit test: all facet fields are populated from session metadata
- Unit test: build_code_artifact_doc sets doc_type to "code_artifacts"

title: CI/CD pipeline
description:
Set up continuous integration and deployment pipeline.

## Scope
- GitHub Actions workflow for CI:
  - cargo fmt --check
  - cargo clippy -- -D warnings
  - cargo test (unit + integration)
  - cargo build --release
- Test matrix: Linux (primary), macOS (secondary)
- Artifact: release binary uploaded on tag push
- Branch protection: CI must pass before merge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI runs on every push and PR
- [ ] Format, lint, test, build all pass
- [ ] Release binary built on tag push
- [ ] CI completes in under 10 minutes
- [ ] Clippy warnings treated as errors

title: Tag extraction: explicit and structural tiers
description:
## Tag Extraction: Explicit and Structural Tiers

Implement tiers 1 and 2 of the three-tier tag extraction pipeline.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create `src/tags.rs` — new module
- Modify `src/main.rs` — add `mod tags;` declaration

### What to Implement
- `pub fn extract_explicit_tags(events: &[CanonicalEvent]) -> Vec<String>`
  - Extract tool names from tool_call events (Edit, Bash, Read, Write, Grep, Glob)
  - Extract language names from code fence markers (```rust -> "rust", ```python -> "python", etc.)
  - Deduplicate and lowercase
- `pub fn extract_structural_tags(events: &[CanonicalEvent]) -> Vec<String>`
  - Map file extensions to languages from file_paths (.rs -> "rust", .py -> "python", .ts -> "typescript", .go -> "go", .jsx -> "react", etc.) — at least 15 extension mappings
  - Extract command names from Bash content (docker, git, npm, cargo, kubectl, make, pip) — word-boundary match on first token of command
  - Extract error type tags from error_fingerprints if present

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib tags::tests
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- Unit test: tool_call event with tool="Edit" produces tag "edit"
- Unit test: code fence ```python produces tag "python"
- Unit test: file_path "src/main.rs" produces tag "rust"
- Unit test: Bash content "docker build ." produces tag "docker"
- Unit test: duplicate tags are deduplicated
- Unit test: all tags are lowercase
- At least 15 file extension -> language mappings defined
close_reason: done

title: Tag extraction pipeline (three-tier)
description:
## Three-Tier Tag Extraction Pipeline

Build the tag extraction system that automatically tags sessions during indexing.

### Three Tiers
1. **Explicit:** Extract tool names from tool_call events (Edit, Bash, Read), languages from code fence markers (```rust -> "rust")
2. **Structural:** File extensions from file_paths (.rs -> "rust", .py -> "python"), command names from Bash content (docker, git, npm, cargo, kubectl), error types from error_fingerprints
3. **Keyword list:** Match content against bundled technology dictionary (~200 terms: frameworks, databases, cloud services, protocols). Exact word-boundary matches only.

### Deliverables
- src/tags.rs module with extract_tags(session_events) -> Vec<String>
- Bundled keyword dictionary file (~200 technology terms)
- Integration with document builder (tags populated at index time)
- Unit tests for each tier

### References
- Phase 2 bead: agentscribe-2dp.1
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md

title: Index management CLI commands (rebuild, stats, optimize)
description:
## Index Management CLI Commands

Implement the agentscribe index subcommand with rebuild, stats, and optimize operations.

### Commands
- agentscribe index rebuild — Recreate the entire Tantivy index from session files in ~/.agentscribe/sessions/
- agentscribe index stats — Show document count, index size on disk, segment count/info
- agentscribe index optimize — Compact segments (merge small segments into larger ones)

### Deliverables
- CLI subcommand registration in src/cli.rs
- Index rebuild: iterate all session .jsonl files, build documents, commit to fresh index
- Index stats: read index metadata, display in human-readable format
- Index optimize: call IndexWriter::merge on segments
- Incremental indexing hook: IndexWriter::add_document() called during scrape (integrate with existing scraper)
- Progress reporting during rebuild (session count, elapsed time)

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md

title: Phase 1: Plugin System, Scraping & Normalization
description:
## Phase 1: Plugin System, Scraping & Normalization

### Objectives
- Implement plugin system with TOML-based scraper definitions
- Build format-specific parsers (JSONL, Markdown, JSON tree)
- Implement session detection strategies (one-file-per-session, timestamp-gap, delimiter)
- Bundle default plugins for Claude Code, Aider, OpenCode, Codex
- Normalize all formats to canonical event schema
- Track scrape state for incremental scrapes
- Implement skip-and-log error handling at every level
- Extract file paths from events (structured + regex fallback)

### CLI Commands
-  - Initialize global config and data directory
-  - Discover and read agent log files
-  - List available plugins
-  - Validate plugin definitions
-  - Show plugin details

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Plugin TOML schema defined and documented
- [ ] JSONL parser with field mapping support
- [ ] Markdown parser with delimiter-based session detection
- [ ] JSON tree parser for OpenCode format
- [ ] Canonical event schema implementation
- [ ] Scrape state tracking (last-byte-offset, last-modified, session IDs)
- [ ] Incremental scraping (seek to offset on re-run)
- [ ] Error handling: skip malformed lines/fields/files with logging
- [ ] File path extraction from tool_call fields
- [ ] File path extraction via regex from content strings
- [ ] Bundled plugins: claude-code.toml, aider.toml, opencode.toml, codex.toml
- [ ] Data directory layout: config.toml, plugins/, sessions/, state/
- [ ] CLI commands functional

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md
- Plugin authoring guide: /home/coding/AgentScribe/plugins/BUILDING_PLUGINS.md
close_reason: Phase 1 complete, closing to unblock downstream phases

title: Search command with BM25, filters, and output modes
description:
## Search Command Implementation

Implement the agentscribe search command — the primary query interface for agents and humans.

### Search Features
- Full-text BM25 search across session content and summaries (default mode)
- Fuzzy search via Tantivy Levenshtein term queries
- Error lookup: --error <pattern> against normalized error fingerprints
- Code search: --code <query> [--lang <lang>] for code artifacts
- Solution-only: --solution-only to return only extracted solutions
- Similar sessions: --like <session-id> using Tantivy MoreLikeThis

### Filter Flags
- --agent (source agent type)
- --project (project path)
- --since / --before (date range)
- --tag (extracted tags)
- --outcome (success/failure/abandoned/unknown)
- --type (doc_type: session or code_artifact)
- --model (model name)

### Output Modes
- Default: human-readable with snippets
- --json: structured JSON output for agent consumption
- --max-results N: limit result count
- --snippet-length N: control snippet size
- --token-budget N: greedy knapsack context packing (fit as many relevant results as possible within N tokens)

### Deliverables
- CLI subcommand in src/cli.rs
- src/search.rs module with query building, result formatting
- Greedy knapsack algorithm for --token-budget
- Unit tests for query construction and result formatting
- Integration test with a small test index

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md (lines 465-510)
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md

title: End-to-end integration test suite
description:
Full pipeline integration tests across all phases.

## Scope
- Fixture data: sample logs for each supported agent type (Claude Code, Aider, OpenCode, Codex)
- End-to-end: config init → scrape → index → search → verify results
- Enrichment validation: outcome detection, error fingerprinting, solution extraction accuracy
- Performance benchmarks: scrape 1000 sessions under 60s, search under 50ms
- Memory budget validation: RSS stays within plan limits
- Regression tests for known edge cases (truncated files, Unicode, empty sessions)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fixture data for all four initial agent types
- [ ] Full pipeline test passes end-to-end
- [ ] Enrichment results validated against known-good data
- [ ] Performance benchmarks documented and passing
- [ ] Memory profiling test verifies RSS limits
- [ ] Edge case regression suite
close_reason: done

title: Phase 4: Daemon Mode & MCP
description:
Implement the long-running daemon for continuous scraping and optional MCP server.

## Deliverables

### Daemon Core
- `agentscribe daemon start|stop|status|run|logs` commands
- File watcher using notify crate (inotify/fswatch) for automatic scraping
- Scrape debounce (default 5s) to avoid thrashing during active sessions
- Incremental index updates (no full rebuild per session)
- PID file management (~/.agentscribe/agentscribe.pid)
- Log file (~/.agentscribe/daemon.log)
- Files: src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/watcher.rs, src/cli/daemon.rs

### MCP Server (Optional)
- Unix socket MCP server at ~/.agentscribe/mcp.sock
- Four tools: agentscribe_search, agentscribe_status, agentscribe_blame, agentscribe_file
- All tools call same core library as CLI
- Config toggle: [daemon] mcp_enabled = false
- Files: src/mcp/mod.rs, src/mcp/tools.rs

### Systemd Integration
- Generate systemd user-level service unit file
- `agentscribe daemon install` to create ~/.config/systemd/user/agentscribe.service
- Files: src/daemon/systemd.rs

## Testing
- Integration test: daemon watches a directory, detects new file, scrapes automatically
- Test debounce: rapid file changes produce single scrape
- Test MCP tools return same results as CLI equivalents
- Test daemon graceful shutdown (SIGTERM)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe daemon start` runs in background, watches all plugin source paths
- New agent log files are automatically scraped within debounce window
- `agentscribe daemon status` shows PID, uptime, RSS, last scrape time
- MCP server (when enabled) responds to all four tool calls correctly
- Daemon RSS stays under 20MB when idle
close_reason: done

title: Daemon core: start/stop/status/run/logs
description:
Implement the daemon lifecycle management commands.

## Scope
- `agentscribe daemon start` — background, writes PID file
- `agentscribe daemon run` — foreground (for systemd)
- `agentscribe daemon stop` — SIGTERM + clean shutdown
- `agentscribe daemon status` — state, RSS, uptime, activity
- `agentscribe daemon logs [-f]` — tail the daemon log
- PID file at ~/.agentscribe/agentscribe.pid
- Log file at ~/.agentscribe/daemon.log
- Tokio async runtime for event loop

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `daemon start` backgrounds and writes PID file
- [ ] `daemon stop` sends SIGTERM and cleans up PID file
- [ ] `daemon status` shows PID, uptime, RSS, peak RSS, session count, last scrape
- [ ] `daemon run` runs in foreground (suitable for systemd)
- [ ] `daemon logs` tails log file, `-f` for follow mode
- [ ] Clean shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
- [ ] Daemon idles under 20MB RSS
close_reason: done

title: File watcher and scrape debounce
description:
Implement inotify-based file watching with debounced scraping.

## Scope
- Use `notify` crate for cross-platform file watching (inotify on Linux)
- Watch all plugin source paths for changes
- Debounce: wait configurable seconds (default 5s) after last change before scraping
- Incremental index updates after ingesting new sessions
- Process one source file at a time (I/O bottleneck, not CPU)
- Stay under 50MB RSS during active scrape

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File watcher detects changes in all configured plugin paths
- [ ] Debounce prevents thrashing during active agent sessions
- [ ] Scrape triggers automatically on file change
- [ ] Index updated incrementally after each scrape
- [ ] Memory stays under 50MB during active scrape
close_reason: Implemented inotify-based file watching with debounced scraping. Extracted Debouncer struct from WatchLoop for clean separation. Added MCP server lifecycle to run_watch_loop. Files scraped one at a time with scraper dropped between batches to keep RSS bounded. 20 daemon unit tests covering Debouncer logic and glob matching. All tests pass.

title: MCP server over Unix socket
description:
Optional MCP server mode exposing four tools via Unix socket.

## Scope
- Unix socket at ~/.agentscribe/mcp.sock (configurable)
- Enabled via config.toml [daemon] mcp_enabled = true
- Four MCP tools calling same core library as CLI:
  1. agentscribe_search — full search with all params
  2. agentscribe_status — plugin list, session counts, daemon state, index stats
  3. agentscribe_blame — file:line → session(s) lookup
  4. agentscribe_file — chronological session list for a file path
- Thin wrapper, not separate implementation
- MCP never required; CLI always works independently

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP server starts when daemon starts with mcp_enabled=true
- [ ] All four tools functional and return same data as CLI equivalents
- [ ] Unix socket cleaned up on daemon shutdown
- [ ] Concurrent MCP requests handled correctly
- [ ] MCP disabled by default (opt-in)
close_reason: Implemented optional MCP server over Unix socket. Four tools via JSON-RPC 2.0: agentscribe_search, agentscribe_status, agentscribe_blame, agentscribe_file. Fix: added mod mcp to main.rs to wire mcp.rs into the module tree. MCP disabled by default, enabled via [daemon] mcp_enabled = true.

title: Systemd service integration
description:
User-level systemd service for the daemon.

## Scope
- Generate ~/.config/systemd/user/agentscribe.service
- ExecStart points to agentscribe daemon run
- Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=5
- CLI command to install/uninstall the service unit
- No root required, no network ports (unless MCP on localhost)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `agentscribe daemon install` creates systemd user service
- [ ] `agentscribe daemon uninstall` removes it
- [ ] Service starts on boot via WantedBy=default.target
- [ ] Restarts automatically on crash
- [ ] Works with `systemctl --user enable/start/stop agentscribe`
close_reason: done

title: Concurrent access and file locking
description:
Ensure safe concurrent access between daemon and CLI commands.

## Scope
- scrape-state.json: flock/fs2::FileExt::lock_exclusive prevents concurrent writes
- Tantivy: native concurrent readers with single writer
- IndexWriter held only during active scrape/commit, not while idle
- CLI read-only commands (search, status, blame, file) never touch scrape state
- Second scrape process waits for lock (no data corruption)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File lock on scrape-state.json during writes
- [ ] Concurrent CLI search works while daemon is scraping
- [ ] Two simultaneous scrape commands don't corrupt state
- [ ] Lock timeout configurable (avoid indefinite wait)
- [ ] Read-only commands never contend with write lock
close_reason: done

title: Tantivy schema definition and index creation
description:
## Tantivy Schema Definition and Index Creation

Implement the Tantivy schema and index open/create logic. This is the first half of agentscribe-y71.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create `src/index.rs` — new module
- Modify `src/main.rs` — add `mod index;` declaration
- Modify `Cargo.toml` — add `tantivy` dependency

### What to Implement
- `pub fn build_schema() -> tantivy::schema::Schema` returning a schema with:
  - Full-text TEXT fields: `content`, `summary`, `solution_summary`, `code_content`
  - Faceted FACET fields: `session_id`, `source_agent`, `project`, `tags`, `outcome`, `error_fingerprint`, `file_paths`, `git_commits`, `doc_type`
  - Code artifact fields: `code_language` (FACET), `code_file_path` (FACET), `code_is_final` (U64)
  - Date field: `timestamp` (DATE)
  - Numeric field: `turn_count` (U64, INDEXED+STORED)
- `pub fn open_or_create_index(path: &Path, schema: &Schema) -> tantivy::Result<Index>` — creates dir if missing, opens existing index or creates new one

### Test Command
```bash
cargo test --lib index::tests
```

### Pass/Fail Criteria
- `build_schema()` returns a Schema with all 16+ fields named above
- `open_or_create_index` creates a new index in a temp dir and returns Ok
- `open_or_create_index` on an existing index dir returns Ok without error
- Unit test: round-trip — create schema, open index, verify all field names exist via `schema.get_field(name)`
- Unit test: index dir is created if it doesn't exist

title: Phase 3: Enrichment & Intelligence
description:
Implement the enrichment pipeline that adds intelligence to scraped sessions.

## Deliverables

### Summaries
- Auto-generate session summaries from first user prompt + outcome + files touched
- `agentscribe summarize` CLI command
- Files: src/enrichment/summary.rs, src/cli/summarize.rs

### Outcome Detection
- Signal-scoring system to classify sessions as success/failure/abandoned/unknown
- Configurable weights in config.toml under [outcome.weights]
- Files: src/enrichment/outcome.rs

### Solution Extraction
- Identify resolution window (last error → user confirmation)
- Extract Edit/Write/Bash tool calls as solution_summary
- Files: src/enrichment/solution.rs

### Error Fingerprinting
- Regex pipeline to extract and normalize error patterns
- Strip variable parts (paths, hosts, ports, PIDs, timestamps, UUIDs)
- Store as error_fingerprint facet in Tantivy
- Files: src/enrichment/errors.rs

### Anti-Pattern Detection
- Analyze failed/abandoned sessions for rejection windows
- Link to successful alternatives via error fingerprint matching
- Store in <session-id>.anti-patterns.jsonl sidecar files
- Files: src/enrichment/antipatterns.rs

### Code Artifact Extraction
- Extract fenced code blocks from assistant responses
- Index as separate Tantivy documents with doc_type: "code_artifact"
- Track language, file path, session ID, code_is_final flag
- Files: src/enrichment/code_artifacts.rs

### Git Commit Correlation
- `git log --after=<start> --before=<end>` during session windows
- Reverse index: commit_hash → session_id
- `agentscribe blame` CLI command
- `agentscribe file` CLI command
- Files: src/enrichment/git.rs, src/cli/blame.rs, src/cli/file.rs

## Testing
- Unit tests for each enrichment module
- Integration tests with sample session data
- Validate fingerprint normalization against known error patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe summarize` generates accurate one-line summaries
- Outcome detection correctly classifies test sessions with known outcomes
- Error fingerprints match across sessions with same error types but different variable parts
- `agentscribe blame src/file.rs:42` links to the correct session
- Anti-patterns include working alternatives when available

title: Tantivy index schema and document builder
description:
## Tantivy Index Schema and Document Builder

Create the Tantivy index with the full schema and implement the document builder that converts normalized sessions into indexed documents.

### Deliverables
- Tantivy index creation with all fields from plan.md:
  - Full-text: content, summary, solution_summary, code_content
  - Faceted: session_id, source_agent, project, tags, outcome, error_fingerprint, file_paths, git_commits, doc_type
  - Code artifacts: code_language, code_file_path, code_is_final
  - Date/numeric: timestamp, turn_count
- Document builder: one document per session, content concatenated with role prefixes
- Content truncation policy: tool_result capped at 1000 chars, total 500KB (trim middle)
- doc_type facet (sessions vs code_artifacts)

### Implementation Notes
- Add tantivy dependency to Cargo.toml
- Create src/index.rs module
- Schema definition as a function returning tantivy::schema::Schema
- Document builder takes a Vec<CanonicalEvent> and produces a tantivy::Document

### References
- Plan: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/plan.md (lines 347-391 for schema)
- Phase 2 bead: agentscribe-2dp.1

title: Status command
description:
## Status Command

Implement the agentscribe status command to show a summary of tracked agents, session counts, index health, and daemon state.

### Output
- Per-agent: name, session count, last scraped timestamp, total events
- Index: document count, size on disk, last rebuild time
- Daemon: running/stopped, PID, uptime (if applicable — daemon is Phase 4)
- Scrape state: number of tracked source files, total bytes processed

### Deliverables
- CLI subcommand registration
- Read from scrape-state.json, index stats, daemon PID file
- Human-readable table output
- --json flag for structured output

### References
- CLI reference: /home/coding/AgentScribe/docs/cli-reference.md

title: Phase 5: SQLite Format Support & Extended Agents
description:
Add SQLite format parser for Cursor, Windsurf, and similar agents.

## Deliverables

### SQLite Format Parser
- Add sqlite format handler to plugin framework (rusqlite read-only)
- Parse cursorDiskKV table structure (composerData + bubbleId keys)
- Handle large databases (25GB+) with read-only mode and page cache limits
- Files: src/parser/sqlite.rs

### Cursor Plugin
- Bundle cursor.toml plugin definition
- Extract sessions from state.vscdb (global + workspace)
- Handle composerData:<composerId> and bubbleId:<composerId>:<bubbleId> keys
- Files: plugins/cursor.toml

### Windsurf Plugin
- Bundle windsurf.toml plugin definition
- Handle 20-conversation hard limit (scrape-early-or-lose-data warning)
- Detect protobuf format in newer versions
- Files: plugins/windsurf.toml

### Community Plugin Examples
- Create plugins/examples/ directory with documented template
- Example: custom-agent.toml with all configuration options annotated

### Git Auto-Commit Integration
- Optionally commit new normalized sessions to git on scrape
- Config: [scrape] git_auto_commit = false
- Files: src/scrape/git_commit.rs

## Testing
- Unit tests with sample state.vscdb fixtures
- Test truncation detection (file shrinks between scrapes)
- Test large database handling (PRAGMA cache_size enforcement)
- Verify Windsurf overwrite detection triggers full rescan

## Acceptance Criteria
- `agentscribe scrape` correctly parses Cursor state.vscdb files
- Windsurf plugin warns about 20-conversation limit in status output
- Custom plugin TOML with format = "sqlite" works without code changes
- Git auto-commit creates one commit per scrape with session count in message
close_reason:
Phase 5 complete - all deliverables already implemented:

1. SQLite parser (src/parser/sqlite.rs): Read-only mode, 8MB cache, key_filter, key_session_id_regex
2. Cursor plugin (plugins/cursor.toml): Global/workspace state.vscdb, bubbleId pattern
3. Windsurf plugin (plugins/windsurf.toml): truncation_limit=20, CLI warning about 20-conv limit
4. Community examples (examples/): plugin_template.toml, sqlite-agent.toml with all options annotated
5. Git auto-commit (src/scraper/mod.rs): [scrape] git_auto_commit config, wired in CLI

Tests: 9 SQLite parser tests pass, 13 scraper tests pass (truncation, git commit verified)

title: SQLite format parser for plugin framework
description:
Add sqlite format type to the scraper plugin system.

## Scope
- New parser type: format = "sqlite" in plugin TOML
- Use rusqlite (bundled) for read-only access (?mode=ro)
- PRAGMA cache_size = -8000 (8MB) per plan memory budget
- Handle state.vscdb files (cursorDiskKV table pattern)
- Incremental: compare file mtime to last_scraped, skip if unchanged
- Truncation detection: if file shrinks (Windsurf overwrites), full rescan
- JSON blob parsing from SQLite values

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SQLite parser reads cursorDiskKV table
- [ ] JSON blobs within SQLite values parsed correctly
- [ ] Incremental scraping via mtime comparison
- [ ] Read-only mode, never modifies source DB
- [ ] Memory stays under 15MB per plan budget
- [ ] Handles 25GB+ global databases (Cursor) without OOM
close_reason: Implemented SQLite format parser: src/parser/sqlite.rs with read-only rusqlite access, PRAGMA cache_size=-8000 (8MB), cursorDiskKV table pattern, key_session_id_regex for per-conversation sessions, JSON blob parsing with content_path navigation. Added cursor.toml and windsurf.toml plugins. Incremental mtime-based skipping via StateManager::needs_rescrape(), truncation detection on file shrink. All 5 SQLite parser tests pass.

title: Cursor and Windsurf scraper plugins
description:
Bundle plugins for Cursor and Windsurf using the SQLite parser.

## Cursor Plugin
- Location: ~/.config/Cursor/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb + workspaceStorage/{hash}/state.vscdb
- Session detection: one key per session (composerData:<composerId>)
- Messages: bubbleId:<composerId>:<bubbleId> keys, ordered by rowid ASC
- Session ID from composerId field
- Two databases: global has content, workspace has UI state
- Size warning: global DB can be 25GB+

## Windsurf Plugin
- Same cursorDiskKV pattern as Cursor
- Location: ~/.config/Windsurf/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb
- CRITICAL: Hard limit of 20 conversations (21st overwrites oldest)
- Must scrape frequently or data is permanently lost
- May use protobuf in newer versions (handle gracefully)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cursor.toml plugin bundled and functional
- [ ] windsurf.toml plugin bundled and functional
- [ ] Both correctly parse composerData and bubbleId keys
- [ ] Windsurf truncation detection triggers full rescan
- [ ] Large Cursor DBs handled without excessive memory

title: Community plugin examples and git auto-commit
description:
Community plugin infrastructure and git integration for session storage.

## Scope
- Community plugin examples directory with template and documentation
- Example plugins for less common agents (Copilot Chat, Continue.dev)
- Git auto-commit: optionally commit new sessions to a git repo on scrape
- Config: [scrape] git_auto_commit = false in config.toml

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] examples/ directory with plugin template and 2+ example plugins
- [ ] Git auto-commit creates commits after successful scrape when enabled
- [ ] Commit messages include session count and agent types
- [ ] Auto-commit disabled by default
- [ ] Plugin authoring guide updated with community contribution workflow
close_reason: Implemented community plugin infrastructure and git auto-commit for session storage: examples/ directory with template and 2 example plugins (copilot-chat, continue-dev), git_auto_commit in scraper/mod.rs, disabled by default, commit messages include session count and agent types, BUILDING_PLUGINS.md updated with community contribution workflow
